tavern_common.name: The Obsidian Flask - Common Room
tavern_common.description: A sprawling, multi-level tavern lit by faintly glowing crystal chandeliers. The air is thick with the scent of spiced wine, roasted wyvern, and the murmur of mercenaries, cutthroats, and scheming merchants.

tavern_cellar.name: The Obsidian Flask - Shadowed Cellar
tavern_cellar.description: Damp stone walls covered in luminescent moss. Enormous casks of darkfire ale line the walls. A faint, unnatural chill emanates from a bricked-up archway at the far end.

tavern_backroom.name: The Obsidian Flask - Velvet Parlor
tavern_backroom.description: A secluded room draped in heavy crimson velvet. A circular oak table sits in the center, scarred with dagger marks and stains of questionable origin. Sound is magically dampened here.

upper_balcony.name: The Obsidian Flask - Upper Balcony
upper_balcony.description: A rickety wooden balcony overlooking the common room below. Shadows pool in the corners, perfect for listening in on the drunken tales of patrons.

obsidian_courtyard.name: Obsidian Courtyard
obsidian_courtyard.description: An open-air courtyard paved with jagged black stones. A dead, twisted tree grows in the center, its branches laden with silent, watching ravens.

market_square.name: The Weeping Market
market_square.description: A bustling, chaotic bazaar where merchants sell everything from cursed artifacts to exotic silks. Criers yell above the din, and pickpockets stalk the distracted crowds.

alchemists_alley.name: Alchemist's Alley
alchemists_alley.description: A narrow, twisting alley choked with strangely colored smog. Bubbling sounds and occasional explosions echo from the shuttered workshops.

hidden_sanctum.name: The Hidden Sanctum
hidden_sanctum.description: A small, heavily warded chamber filled with ancient tomes, shifting starmaps, and glass jars containing preserved, glowing organs. Dust motes dance in the magical ambient light.

gareth_barkeep.name: Gareth Ironhand
gareth_barkeep.description: A massive figure missing his left eye. His right arm is a complex prosthesis of brass and iron. He fiercely polishes a glass while scanning the room.
gareth_barkeep.personality: Gruff, pragmatic, and fiercely protective of his tavern. He speaks in a low rumble. He hates magic but respects coin and physical strength. Very observant.
knowledge: A smuggler named Vane was asking about an ancient relic three nights ago.
The Velvet Parlor is currently rented by the Silk Cloaks, a dangerous local gang.
The bricked-up arch in the cellar sometimes whispers when the moon is new.
Lira owes a massive debt to the Silk Cloaks.
secrets: Gareth used to be an enforcer for the Silk Cloaks before he lost his arm and eye.
He has a hidden compartment under the bar holding a masterwork hand-cannon.

lira_barmaid.name: Lira
lira_barmaid.description: A wiry, fast-moving woman with vividly dyed purple hair. She wears a patchwork leather vest with too many pockets.
lira_barmaid.personality: Cynical, razor-sharp wit, and dangerously observant. She plays the fool but is constantly calculating escape routes and angles.
knowledge: Vane left a locked wooden puzzle box with her before he vanished.
The ravens in the Obsidian Courtyard are familiars belonging to the mad alchemist, Vorn.
Players of high status or wealth are targets for pickpockets in the Weeping Market.
secrets: Lira is actively looking to sell Vane's puzzle box to the highest bidder to pay off her debt.
She knows a secret path over the rooftops from the Upper Balcony to Alchemist's Alley.

vorn_alchemist.name: Vorn the Mad
vorn_alchemist.description: An ancient, rail-thin elf smelling of brimstone and lotus. His robes are singed, and his eyes wildly dart around the room. He often speaks to people who aren't there.
vorn_alchemist.personality: Erratic, paranoid, and brilliant. He transitions rapidly from whispering conspiratorially to shouting. He constantly references cosmic horrors and unseen forces.
knowledge: The puzzle box Lira has contains a shard of a shattered god's soul.
The whispering in the cellar is the resting place of a bound demon.
Magic is unraveling in this city, caused by the Silk Cloaks' reckless rituals.
secrets: Vorn caused the explosion that killed his apprentice, a fact that torments him to insanity.
He is preparing to open the arch in the cellar to harness the demon's power.

silas_thief.name: Silas
silas_thief.description: A charming, unnervingly handsome half-elf in a pristine white coat. He nervously flips a gold coin between his knuckles.
silas_thief.personality: Smooth-talking, overly friendly, and highly manipulative. He always tries to seem helpful while figuring out how to exploit someone. Fearful of the Silk Cloaks.
knowledge: The Silk Cloaks have placed a bounty on Vane's head, dead or alive.
Gareth's iron iron hand was custom-built by Vorn.
There is a hidden entrance to the Velvet Parlor from the Upper Balcony.
secrets: Silas is the one who revealed Vane's location to the Silk Cloaks.
Silas is terrified that Lira will figure out his betrayal.

house_wine_bottle.name: Bottle of Darkfire Ale
house_wine_bottle.description: A heavy stone jug. The contents are pitch black and slightly warm to the touch. It smells like roasted malt and danger.

torn_note.name: Blood-Stained Ledger
torn_note.description: A small leather-bound book. The last entry reads: 'Vorn demands the puzzle box. The cellar must be opened.'

puzzle_box.name: Vane's Puzzle Box
puzzle_box.description: A cube of intricate, interlocking gears made of an unknown, iridescent metal. It hums faintly.

brass_key.name: Heavy Brass Key
brass_key.description: An oversized key that smells of soot, presumably for the Velvet Parlor.

rules: NPCs will naturally weave elements of their surroundings and the player's identity (Age, Gender, Occupation) into their dialogue.
NPCs only reveal secrets if trust >= 60 or if the player presents compelling, context-aware evidence.
The player's occupation profoundly impacts how characters treat them. For example, intellectuals or scholars will intrigue Vorn, while Gareth respects soldiers or laborers.
Violence immediately raises world tension and permanently lowers trust with Gareth.
Lira will outright refuse to speak about the puzzle box unless bribed or physically intimidated.
If the player knows about Silas's betrayal, Silas will attempt to flee or lie frantically.
The demon behind the cellar archway cannot be accessed without unbinding the magical wards (requires high trust with Vorn or a specific artifact).
